mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows or positive return frequency drops below 55 percent during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.0 percent, labor market slack suppresses corporate earnings growth expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in over 60 percent of observations when UNRATE exceeds 8.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts signals broad-based consumer demand recovery, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to yield a positive return frequency above 58 percent over 60-day horizons.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in industrial production trigger immediate risk-off positioning and liquidity tightening, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO growth coincides with positive 5-day forward returns in more than 55 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate income and corporate revenue projections, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rolling Rank IC remains below 0.01 over 3-year windows or positive return frequency drops below 52 percent during payroll expansions.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index rises above 230, inflation expectations force monetary tightening fears, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in over 65 percent of periods when CPIAUCSL exceeds 230.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising real GDP and falling industrial production indicates inventory buildup and weak final demand, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The GDPC1-INDPRO divergence signal yields a positive return frequency above 57 percent over 60-day horizons.

mechanism_narrative: Near-zero policy rates combined with elevated unemployment create a liquidity trap environment where equity markets decouple from traditional discount rate models, producing negative short-term returns due to risk aversion.
regime_hint: liquidity trap or zero lower bound
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 60 percent of observations when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25 percent and UNRATE exceeds 8.0 percent.
